title: Praxis School Counselor (5422) — 200 Source Questions + 300 Guided Review
description: Contains 200 source diagnostic questions and 300 source-derived guided-review tasks; the assistant audit found 100 distinct source content kernels. Two independently authored 100-question diagnostic batches linked to a 12-chapter learning library, targeted skill practice, and an optional 120-question timed simulation. The official 5422 test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; AlloFlow practice is not an official form or score.
contentReview: Assistant audit completed: the 200 source questions contain 100 distinct content kernels and 100 parallel variants under the normalized answer-set/rationale/reference test. The 300 additional activities are assistant-reviewed guided reasoning transformations, not independent exam questions. The 500-distinct-question target is not met.
disclaimer: Independent preparation material. Not affiliated with or endorsed by ETS or ASCA. The official Praxis School Counselor (5422) test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; the 100-item diagnostic batches are not official-length forms. Practice results are not official or scaled Praxis scores, pass predictions, credentials, mental-health treatment, legal advice, or substitutes for current federal, state, local, district, crisis, mandated-reporting, and ethical requirements.

items.prompt: A principal asks the school counselor to describe the central purpose of a comprehensive school counseling program. Which response is best?
choices: It provides planned academic, career, and social/emotional supports that improve access and outcomes for every student.
It primarily offers therapy to students with diagnosed mental-health disorders.
It exists mainly to complete scheduling and testing paperwork for administrators.
It serves only students referred because of severe behavior concerns.
items.rationale: A comprehensive program is preventive, developmental, data informed, and available to all students across academic, career, and social/emotional development.
choiceRationales: Correct. A comprehensive program is preventive, developmental, data informed, and available to all students across academic, career, and social/emotional development.

Not the best answer. "It primarily offers therapy to students with diagnosed mental-health disorders." misses or conflicts with the controlling school-counseling principle. A comprehensive program is preventive, developmental, data informed, and available to all students across academic, career, and social/emotional development.

Not the best answer. "It exists mainly to complete scheduling and testing paperwork for administrators." misses or conflicts with the controlling school-counseling principle. A comprehensive program is preventive, developmental, data informed, and available to all students across academic, career, and social/emotional development.

Not the best answer. "It serves only students referred because of severe behavior concerns." misses or conflicts with the controlling school-counseling principle. A comprehensive program is preventive, developmental, data informed, and available to all students across academic, career, and social/emotional development.

items.prompt: A counselor is assigned to coordinate all standardized testing for three weeks, leaving no time for counseling services. What is the most appropriate response?
choices: Accept the assignment permanently because administrators define every counselor duty.
Use role and time-use data to explain the impact, propose an appropriate staffing solution, and advocate for counseling services.
Refuse publicly without discussing student impact or possible solutions.
Cancel only services for students who have not requested individual counseling.
items.rationale: Testing coordination and routine discipline supervision are generally inappropriate noncounseling assignments; advocacy should be collaborative, documented, and centered on student access.

Hide the result to protect program credibility.
Select a different outcome after seeing the data and call it the original goal.
Blame students or families for the shortfall.
items.rationale: Transparent reporting builds trust and learning. A missed goal can guide improvement when evidence, limitations, and next decisions are communicated without blame.
choiceRationales: Correct. Transparent reporting builds trust and learning. A missed goal can guide improvement when evidence, limitations, and next decisions are communicated without blame.

assistantReview.limitation: Normalized answer-set, rationale, and reference reuse is counted as one content kernel. bankDisclosure: This pack has 500 learning activities, not 500 independent exam questions: 200 source questions (100 distinct content kernels; 100 parallel variants) plus 300 source-derived guided-review activities. 400 newly authored independent questions are needed to reach the 500-question target.
